results.detailed_description: You were seeing the bottom of the previous item's playback progress bar peeking over the top of the item you're focused on. That's because the Discover feed stacked its full-screen video pages with no gap between them, and each card's progress bar sits near the card's bottom edge — so the tail of the card above poked into view at the top. I added a 72pt gutter between the pages (LazyVStack spacing 0 to 72). When a card is settled on screen that gutter is scrolled off the top, so it changes nothing about how a single card looks or how paging feels; it just pushes the card above up far enough that its progress bar clears the top of the screen, leaving clean background there instead of a stray bar. I couldn't run it on-device here (agents don't build — vigil deploys the pushed commit), so please confirm on the phone that the previous item's bar is gone from the top and the swipe between items still feels right. If the leftover strip is still visible or the gap between cards feels too large while swiping, I can nudge the 72 up or down.
results.identified_issue: Root cause: the feed's LazyVStack used spacing: 0, and each page is a full-height video card whose read-only playback bar (PlaybackIndicator + time labels in NowPlayingContent.progress) sits ~16pt above the card's bottom edge. Because the view-aligned settled card lands a small distance below the very top of the feed (top inset), the flush card above pokes its bottom ~40-50pt into that gap, so you saw the previous item's progress bar hanging over the top. Fix: LazyVStack(spacing: 72). The gutter is scrolled off-screen above the settled card (viewAligned + scrollPosition anchor .top), so it costs nothing while you're on a card; it just pushes the previous card up past the fold, leaving clean background at the top instead of its bar. Chose 72 to clear the observed ~50pt tail with margin without making the black band between cards feel large mid-swipe. Alternative rejected: eliminating the top inset via ignoresSafeArea, which risks shoving current-card content under the status bar and is a broader change than the reporter asked for. Not verified on-device (agents don't build; vigil deploys).
results.summary: Added a gutter between Discover feed pages so the card above stops peeking over the top

description: I can see the bottom of the progress bar of the previous item so add some more spacing between items so that when we're focused on a singular item we don't see the bottom of the progress bar up above it. I've attached a screen shot so you can see.
